title: SENIOR HEALTH INSURANCE BENEFITS ASSISTANCE - 211info
text: CLACKAMAS COUNTY SOCIAL SERVICES SENIOR HEALTH INSURANCE BENEFITS ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides trained volunteers who educate, assist, and serve as advocates for people with Medicare. Volunteers help people understand their rights and options for health insurance so that they can make informed decisions. They can also provide information on the Qualified Medicare Beneficiary (QMB) program, a Medicaid program that helps people with Medicare pay for their medical care. 2051 Kaen Road #135 Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Individuals with Medicare and family members or caregivers Hours: Monday-Thursday 7am-6pm (503) 655-8269 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 655-8269 INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or visit website for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Call for information LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Clackamas County SITE HOURS: Monday-Thursday 7am-6pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Located at the intersection of Kaen and Beavercreek Roads, at the top of the hill in Oregon City. On TriMet Bus Line 33 with sign: Clackamas Community College/CCC Services: Health Insurance Claims Assistance for Beneficiaries Medicaid Information/Counseling Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - ADMINISTRATION COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - AREA AGENCY ON AGING HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - AGING AND DISABILITY RESOURCE CONNECTION INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OPTIONS COUNSELING INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE–MEDICAID INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - SENIOR COMPANION PROGRAM INFORMATION SERVICES - FAMILY CAREGIVER SUPPORT PROGRAM AND RELATIVES AS PARENTS REHABILITATION/HABILITATION SERVICES - DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES TRANSPORTATION - TRANSPORTATION REACHING PEOPLE UTILITIES - UTILITY SUPPORT If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. CLACKAMAS COUNTY SOCIAL SERVICES
